mnemonics: Every Sequence Eventually Settles (E.S.E.S.): In a sequentially compact space, every sequence has a subsequence that eventually settles/converges to a point in the space.
keyPoints: A metric space is sequentially compact if every sequence has a convergent subsequence.
Sequential compactness is equivalent to compactness (every open cover has a finite subcover) in metric spaces.
The Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem states that every bounded sequence in R^n has a convergent subsequence, making closed and bounded subsets of R^n sequentially compact.
commonMistakes: Confusing 'every sequence has a convergent subsequence' with 'every sequence converges'. A space can have non-convergent sequences but still be sequentially compact if those sequences have convergent subsequences.
Assuming that because a space is closed, it is sequentially compact. A closed subset of a metric space must also be bounded (and the space complete) for sequential compactness.
Forgetting that the limit of the convergent subsequence must lie within the space itself, not just any limit point.
